name: Type 2 Diabetes - Path 4: Patients on T2DM Meds with Elevated A1c (No Diagnosis)
description: Tests the hardest T2DM identification path: patients who have NO formal diabetes diagnosis but ARE prescribed diabetes medications AND have elevated HbA1c. This requires a cross-resource query combining MedicationRequest and Observation evidence at the Patient level. Tests whether the LLM can use _has parameters or other cross-resource FHIR search techniques.
prompt: Find patients who are on type 2 diabetes medications and also have elevated hemoglobin A1c results above 6.5 percent, even if they do not have a formal diabetes diagnosis.

prompts.naive: Find patients who are on type 2 diabetes medications and also have elevated hemoglobin A1c results above 6.5 percent, even if they do not have a formal diabetes diagnosis.
prompts.expert: Find all patients who are currently prescribed type 2 diabetes medications such as metformin, sulfonylureas, or thiazolidinediones, and who also have hemoglobin A1c lab results at or above 6.5 percent. These patients may not have a formal diabetes diagnosis recorded.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path 4: No T1DM dx, no T2DM dx, but T2DM medication prescribed + abnormal lab. Requires combining evidence from MedicationRequest and Observation. The most challenging test case.

expected_query.notes: This test requires two queries: one for T2DM medications and one for elevated A1c. The expected patient set is the INTERSECTION of patients from both queries who do NOT have a T2D diagnosis. Acceptable approaches include dual _has on Patient, or separate queries with client-side intersection.
